xBRIEFInfo.description: Scope xBRIEF ingested from GitHub issue #61

plan.title: Break notifications should stack instead of clobbering

narratives.Description: Break notifications should stack instead of clobbering
narratives.Overview:
## Summary

Break banners currently show **one at a time**. When overlapping break types fire, a new break replaces the active banner instead of stacking, so the earlier prompt can be lost before the user acts on it.

Related: #59 (I did. / tallies), #2 (break prompts).

## Current behavior

In `NoisePlayer`, UI state is a single slot:

- `activeBreak: ActiveBreak | null`
- `BreakEngine.setOnFire` → `setActiveBreak({ kind, message })` (replaces any prior banner)
- Dismiss / snooze clear that one slot

`BreakBanner` renders at most one prompt. The engine can fire different kinds on overlapping schedules; same-kind same-instant UI fires are deduped in `BreakEngine`, but **different kinds (or a later fire while a banner is still up) clobber** the visible banner.

OS notifications use `tag: relaxanator-break-${kind}` (per-kind replace), which is separate from the in-app banner clobber.

## Expected behavior

When multiple break types are due while a banner is still active, notifications should **stack** (queue or multi-banner) so earlier prompts are not silently replaced. User can dismiss/snooze each independently (or clear the stack in a deliberate way).

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Firing break type B while banner A is still visible does not discard A without user action
- [ ] Stacked/queued breaks remain actionable (dismiss / snooze per item, or equivalent clear UX)
- [ ] Behavior is predictable when several kinds fire close together
- [ ] (Optional) Align OS notification tagging with stacking intent if in-app and system surfaces should match

## Notes

Stacking was not implemented in the original single-banner design — treat as enhancement unless product intent was multi-banner all along.
narratives.Requirements:
FR-1: Firing break type B while banner A is visible must not discard A without user action.
FR-2: Each stacked banner remains independently actionable (I did. / Snooze / Dismiss apply only to that banner's kind).
FR-3: Stack behavior is deterministic for concurrent kinds (one entry per kind; same-kind re-fire refreshes message in place).
NFR-1: OS Notification tags remain per-kind (`relaxanator-break-${kind}`) — already aligned; no change required.
narratives.LockedDecisions: Refinement 2026-07-14 (#61 triage/implement, operator lock): (1) Replace single activeBreak with an ActiveBreak[] stack. (2) Clean list: at most one banner per break kind; append new kinds, refresh same-kind in place. (3) I did. / Snooze / Dismiss are kind-scoped and remove only that banner. (4) Works with #59 tallies (I did. increments that kind only). (5) Optional OS notification AC already satisfied by per-kind tags — out of scope for code change.

Stacked/queued breaks remain actionable (dismiss / snooze per item, or equivalent clear UX)
Behavior is predictable when several kinds fire close together
(Optional) Align OS notification tagging with stacking intent if in-app and system surfaces should match
